framework.name: OWASP API Security Top 10
framework.disclaimer: OWASP content (CC BY-SA 4.0), summarized with attribution. Not endorsed by OWASP. See the official OWASP project for authoritative text.

controls.title: Broken Object Level Authorization
controls.statement: An API exposes object identifiers without verifying the caller is authorized for that specific object, enabling unauthorized data access.

controls.title: Broken Authentication
controls.statement: Authentication mechanisms are implemented incorrectly, letting attackers assume other identities.

controls.title: Broken Object Property Level Authorization
controls.statement: Excessive data exposure or mass assignment lets callers read or modify properties they should not.

controls.title: Unrestricted Resource Consumption
controls.statement: Missing rate and resource limits allow denial of service or cost-based abuse.

controls.title: Broken Function Level Authorization
controls.statement: Authorization checks miss at the function/endpoint level, exposing privileged operations.

controls.title: Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
controls.statement: Business flows are exposed without protection against automated abuse.

controls.title: Server Side Request Forgery
controls.statement: An API fetches a remote resource from a user-supplied URL without validation, enabling SSRF.

controls.title: Security Misconfiguration
controls.statement: Insecure defaults, incomplete configurations, or verbose errors weaken the API.

controls.title: Improper Inventory Management
controls.statement: Undocumented or outdated API versions and endpoints widen the attack surface.

controls.title: Unsafe Consumption of APIs
controls.statement: Trusting third-party API data without validation propagates their weaknesses into your system.
